title: FTP-1: A Generalist Foundation Tactile Policy Across Tactile Sensors for Contact-Rich Manipulation
summary: Despite the success of vision-based generalist robotic policies, existing tactile-based policies remain tied to fixed embodiments and sensor setups, because tactile signals are highly heterogeneous across hardware. FTP-1 is the first generalist foundation tactile policy, pretrained to acquire transferable tactile manipulation abilities across diverse sensors and embodiments: it supports image-, array-, and state-based tactile inputs via heterogeneous encoders that project them into unified morphology-aware latent tokens, jointly modeled by a shared tactile Transformer expert. Pretrained on around 3,000 hours of tactile manipulation data aggregated from 26 sources spanning human and robot demonstrations across 21 sensors, FTP-1 learns skills that transfer beyond the sensors seen during pretraining. Across finetuning experiments over 5 hardware configurations it improves contact-rich manipulation on seen sensor setups by +17.2% and transfers to two previously unseen tactile-sensor setups with a +31% gain in success rate, establishing the first unified foundation baseline for tactile manipulation.
